query: SpaceX Starbase Pad 2 deluge tests Ship 40 Booster 20 static fire Flight 13
a_issues: Content A is dominated by Instagram and YouTube/social posts rather than solid textual reporting. It does include the key Space.com result, but several top results are low-reliability snippets and one result says Booster 20 rolled to Pad 2 on July 15, which conflicts with the anchored reporting that it was at the pad and static-fired on July 10. The deluge-test fact is only weakly implied via an Instagram snippet rather than clearly documented in a reliable article.
b_issues: Content B has only two results, and one Spaceflight Now snippet includes likely irrelevant or contaminated text about Orion/Blue Moon software testing, reducing trust in that snippet. It also does not explicitly mention Pad 2 deluge tests in the returned snippets.
reasoning: B better supports a correct answer because it contains the most important anchored facts in a cleaner, more reliable form: Space.com explicitly states Booster 20 completed a 33-engine static fire on July 10 ahead of Flight 13, identifies Ship 40 as the upper stage for Flight 13, and notes the test duration. The Spaceflight Now result also confirms the Flight 13 pairing of Booster 20 and Ship 40. A has some of the same facts, but its result set is noisier and less reliable overall, relying heavily on Instagram/YouTube snippets and containing a timeline inconsistency about Booster 20 rolling to Pad 2 on July 15 after the anchored July 10 static fire. Neither set is great on the specific Pad 2 deluge-test detail, but B is still the better evidence set for answering the query correctly.
